title: alchemilla palmata subsp. minor
intro: Alchemilla palmata subsp. minor, also known as the lesser lady's mantle, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the Rosaceae family, characterized by its small, five-petaled flowers and hairy, rounded leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Alchemilla palmata subsp. minor include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Alchemilla palmata subsp. minor is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support women's health and promote overall well-being.
